context.summary: A radio-controlled remote alarm so when you know someoneГўВҖВҷs turned the lights on ГўВҖВ“ or opened a drawer or bag.
how_it_works: Like the Sunlight sensor project, this uses the micro:bitГўВҖВҷs LEDs as a light sensor to measure how much light is falling on the display.

Using a loop, every 10 seconds the sensor program sends a radio message to say if itГўВҖВҷs light or dark. It uses selection to transmit a ГўВҖВҳlights onГўВҖВҷ message if the light measurement is more than (>) 50, and ГўВҖВҳlights offГўВҖВҷ if itГўВҖВҷs less than (<) 50. You may need to change these values depending on the lighting conditions where you are.
Pressing input button A shows a light measurement on the LED display output which will help you set up your alarm, so you can change 50 to the best number for your environment.

The alarm program reacts whenever it gets a message. If the message is equal to ГўВҖВҳlights offГўВҖВҷ then it clears the LED display, but if it is equal to ГўВҖВҳlights onГўВҖВҷ then it shows an angry face and plays the ГўВҖВҳBADDYГўВҖВҷ alarm sound.
Make sure your sensor and alarm use the same radio group ГўВҖВ“ any number between 0 and 255.
improve_it: Combine this with the Tilt alarm project to sense when something is being moved OR if the lights go on.
Make the alarm work the other way so it goes off if someone turns the lights off.
Adapt it to monitor energy use: use a variable to track the number of seconds the lights are left on or trigger an alarm only after theyГўВҖВҷve been on too long.
